 {"id":"bd-1007","title":"Handle FORCE_COLOR=0 in terminal detection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:40:18.827687549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:42:47.276044902Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:42:47.275979971Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-11gq","title":"Tree node markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Tree nodes may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests to verify and document behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Tree node labels with markup - verify if parsed
- [ ] Test Tree with styled branches
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output (if markup should work)
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output
- [ ] Document the expected API clearly

## Implementation Notes
- Check Tree implementation for markup handling
- Add explicit tests to lock down current behavior
- May need Tree::add_node_markup() if markup isn't supported

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/tree_tests.rs  
- src/renderables/tree.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:52.753560616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526581194Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526523125Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024268324Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:46Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899416368Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899375451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1365","title":"demo_showcase: Pretty/Inspect scene","description":"Create a scene that demonstrates Pretty + Inspect on real structs used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.747083708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28.157644052Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pretty"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.762578230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-1365","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Use `Pretty` and `Inspect` on *real demo structs* so the output feels practical:
- `Config`
- `DemoState` snapshot
- a fake API request struct

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is readable and styled (e.g., via panels) rather than raw `Debug`.
- Demonstrates both `Pretty::new(&value)` and `inspect(&console, &value)` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1388","title":"demo_showcase: DemoState model","description":"Define DemoState: service statuses, pipeline steps, progress values, log ring buffer, timestamps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:47.996627782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10.909170452Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:48.012575295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-1388","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Data model

Keep the state small but expressive:
- run id / seed
- start time + elapsed
- services: name -> status (OK/WARN/ERR) + latency + version
- pipeline: ordered stages with progress + status + eta
- logs: ring buffer of recent lines (already formatted strings or structured records)
- current “headline” action text

## Acceptance criteria

- State can be snapshot-read without blocking (Arc<Mutex<…>> or similar).
- State updates drive visible changes in the dashboard each tick.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-13i9","title":"demo_showcase: Scene trait + registry","description":"Define a Scene abstraction (name/summary/run) and a registry for list/select.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.895960587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35.618073665Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scenes"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.908657832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scene API shape

Keep scenes simple and testable. Suggested shape:

- `fn name(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn summary(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn run(&self, console: &Arc<Console>, cfg: &Config) -> Result<(), DemoError>`

A registry returns an ordered list for full runs and a lookup map for `--scene`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--list-scenes` prints name + summary in a nice table (itself a showcase).
- Scene ordering is stable and matches storyboard.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35Z"}]}
@@ -11,9 +11,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-1by","title":"Expand README architecture + feature parity sections","description":"Add clearer render pipeline diagram/steps and explicit feature parity status vs Python Rich (implemented vs not yet).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:49.596493332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:41.993444984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:41.993040282Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1by8","title":"demo_showcase: palette + Theme definitions","description":"Define the palette and implement Theme style definitions (named styles used across scenes).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:58.915479693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:13.025051565Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1by8","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:58.927882861Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-1by8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output

- A single `Theme` definition that includes all named styles the demo uses.
- Style names should be descriptive and reusable, e.g.:
  - `brand.title`, `brand.subtitle`
  - `status.ok`, `status.warn`, `status.err`
  - `section.rule`, `panel.title`, `table.header`
  - `log.trace`/`log.debug`/… (even if logger has its own styling)

## Acceptance criteria

- Most scene styling references these names via markup (`[status.ok]OK[/]`) or theme lookup.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cdx","title":"demo_showcase: timing/performance tuning","description":"Tune timing and refresh rates so the demo feels smooth but not slow; ensure --quick is truly fast.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:25.545745514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:46.774616470Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cdx","depends_on_id":"bd-6ozb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:25.560089945Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-1cdx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Tuning targets

- Default run: “feels animated” but not long (aim ~8–15s interactive).
- `--quick`: near-instant (aim < 3s).
- Refresh rate: cap to something sane (e.g. <= 10 fps).

Ensure sleeps are routed through a single helper so tuning is easy.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:46Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1gou","title":"demo_showcase: shared Console builder","description":"Build the shared Console from Config (theme, width/height overrides, markup/emoji/links, force_terminal).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.491588228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:27.375380285Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["console","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.502866179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-1gou","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Console builder responsibilities

- Apply the demo `Theme` (from the design bead).
- Respect width/height overrides for deterministic output.
- Respect `--force-terminal` to enable color in pipes when requested.
- Provide toggles for markup/emoji/links if we expose them.

## Acceptance criteria

- All scenes use the same Console instance (or share an Arc) so output is consistent.
- Console config is derived solely from `Config` (no hidden env-only behavior except documented defaults like `FORCE_COLOR`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:27Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1gou","title":"demo_showcase: shared Console builder","description":"Build the shared Console from Config (theme, width/height overrides, markup/emoji/links, force_terminal).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.491588228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:53.701008929Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["console","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:51.202039996Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:53.700981077Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.502866179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-1gou","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Console builder responsibilities

- Apply the demo `Theme` (from the design bead).
- Respect width/height overrides for deterministic output.
- Respect `--force-terminal` to enable color in pipes when requested.
- Provide toggles for markup/emoji/links if we expose them.

## Acceptance criteria

- All scenes use the same Console instance (or share an Arc) so output is consistent.
- Console config is derived solely from `Config` (no hidden env-only behavior except documented defaults like `FORCE_COLOR`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jk8","title":"demo_showcase: scene runner main loop","description":"Implement the scene runner for full sequence + --scene single-run mode; ensure clean termination.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.660057261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:50.455414413Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.672997309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Responsibilities

- Initialize console + theme.
- Decide run mode (full vs single scene).
- Ensure clean teardown for live/screen modes (cursor restored, screen disabled).
- Provide a consistent “section framing” between scenes (rule + title).

## Acceptance criteria

- `--scene hero` runs only that scene and exits 0.
- Full run prints a summary/outro and exits 0.
- Errors are printed via rich_rust (e.g., traceback scene) and still exit non-zero when appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1o8x","title":"demo_showcase: Config + CLI parser implementation","description":"Implement a minimal CLI parser and Config struct used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.166796577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:19.977727326Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.178894306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation notes

- Prefer a small `Config` struct with defaults and a `parse_args(Vec<String>) -> Result<Config, String>` style API.
- Keep parsing strict but friendly: unknown flags show error + hint to `--help`.
- Avoid adding heavy dependencies (e.g., clap) for the demo.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--help` prints usage + examples.
- `--list-scenes` and `--scene <name>` parse correctly.
- Boolean flags support both `--foo` and `--no-foo` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:19Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1o8x","title":"demo_showcase: Config + CLI parser implementation","description":"Implement a minimal CLI parser and Config struct used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.166796577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:48.539112863Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:48.539079260Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:46.096304063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.178894306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation notes

- Prefer a small `Config` struct with defaults and a `parse_args(Vec<String>) -> Result<Config, String>` style API.
- Keep parsing strict but friendly: unknown flags show error + hint to `--help`.
- Avoid adding heavy dependencies (e.g., clap) for the demo.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--help` prints usage + examples.
- `--list-scenes` and `--scene <name>` parse correctly.
- Boolean flags support both `--foo` and `--no-foo` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ohr","title":"Port rich.pretty + rich.inspect equivalents","description":"Implement Rich-style pretty printing / inspection.

Scope:
- Provide a Pretty renderable for Rust values (Debug) with optional syntax highlighting
- Provide an inspect-like helper that prints type info + fields when possible

Acceptance criteria:
- Pretty output is stable and width-aware
- Unit tests cover nested structs/collections
- Document Rust-idiomatic differences vs Python inspect
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:55.795289624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:46:35.895142548Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:46:35.895076846Z","close_reason":"Implemented Debug-based Pretty/Inspect renderables","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ohr","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:01.234951263Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pc","title":"Refactor Segment to use Cow<'a, str>","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T01:17:57.773069201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T01:32:28.193304293Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T01:32:28.193244581Z","close_reason":"Completed refactor of Segment to use Cow<'a, str>","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1png","title":"demo_showcase: streaming logs pane","description":"Implement a log tail pane (bounded buffer) that updates during Live runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:00.066582972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:50.750114077Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1png","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:00.082034511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-1png","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Log pane rules

- Keep a bounded ring buffer (e.g. last 10–20 lines).
- Include levels + timestamps (or at least consistent prefixes).
- Use markup/named styles for emphasis (e.g., highlight GET/POST, errors).

## Acceptance criteria

- Log output integrates cleanly with Live (no broken cursor state).
- In non-live mode, print a small representative slice rather than every tick.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:50Z"}]}
@@ -109,7 +109,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2175","title":"demo_showcase: core renderables showcase scenes","description":"Create scenes that exercise core renderables (Table/Panel/Rule/Tree/Columns/Align/Padding/Emoji) with real-world content.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:54.414486281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:35.167863955Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","renderables"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2175","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:35.167837065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2175","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:32.654972566Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2175","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:54.431275454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-2175","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

These scenes are the “bread and butter” proof that rich_rust matches Python Rich’s day-to-day power:

- **Rule**: section dividers + styled rules + alignment
- **Panel**: titles/subtitles, multiple box styles, nested panels, padding
- **Table**: auto sizing, alignment, header styles, status badges, ASCII fallback
- **Tree**: guides styles, icons/emoji, collapsed nodes
- **Columns**: multi-column summary blocks
- **Align + Padding**: centered hero text; padded callouts
- **Emoji**: `:shortcode:` replacement + `Emoji` renderable

## Acceptance criteria

- Content should be realistic: service health, config summaries, release checklists, etc.
- Demonstrate both markup-based styling and programmatic `Text`/`Style` building.
- Make narrow-width behavior look intentional (e.g., fewer columns, truncated text with ellipsis).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21ef","title":"demo_showcase: Syntax deep-dive scene","description":"Implement the Syntax scene (config/code) with line numbers and a theme selection.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:12.225519054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:29.033071314Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","syntax"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21ef","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:12.238288297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-21ef","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Show a config or code snippet that feels relevant to the dashboard story:
- `deploy.toml` / `pipeline.yml` / Rust snippet of a handler

Demonstrate:
- line numbers
- theme selection (e.g., “Solarized (dark)”)
- width handling (wrapping/cropping)

## Feature gating

If `syntax` is disabled, show a styled panel with the exact enable command.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21uy","title":"Panel markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Panel titles and content may have similar markup parsing issues as Table cells. Need explicit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Panel::new() with markup in title - verify markup is parsed
- [ ] Test Panel with markup in content - verify markup is parsed  
- [ ] Test Panel with nested markup styles
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output

## Implementation Notes
- Check if Panel already parses markup correctly
- If not, may need to add Panel::from_markup() similar to Cell
- Add tests either way to document and lock down behavior

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs
- src/renderables/panel.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:50.977751990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036711554Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:11.036694292Z","close_reason":"Created tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs with 14 tests covering: Panel::from_text() does not parse markup, title/subtitle do not parse markup, pre-styled Text preserves styles via from_rich_text(), nested styles work correctly, ANSI codes present in styled output, raw markup tags absent in pre-styled content. Documents that Panel behavior is consistent with Cell (markup not auto-parsed).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-23h8","title":"demo_showcase: CLI flags + help text","description":"Define CLI flags, defaults, and help text; ensure flags map cleanly to runtime configuration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:15.093133590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:28.008688003Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:28.008625547Z","close_reason":"Completed (CLI contract + help text specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23h8","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:15.117557280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-23h8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Requirements

- Provide a human-friendly help output that reads like a real CLI (`demo_showcase --help`).
- Flags must be reflected in a `Config` struct used by all scenes.

## Suggested flags (baseline)

- `--scene <name>` / `--list-scenes`
- `--quick` / `--speed <f64>`
- `--interactive/--no-interactive`
- `--live/--no-live`
- `--screen/--no-screen`
- `--width <usize>` / `--height <usize>`
- `--force-terminal`
- `--export` / `--export-dir <path>`

## Acceptance criteria

- Unknown flags yield a concise error and `--help` hint.
- `--scene` accepts only known scene names (with a helpful list on error).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:38Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-23h8","title":"demo_showcase: CLI flags + help text","description":"Define CLI flags, defaults, and help text; ensure flags map cleanly to runtime configuration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:15.093133590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:33.173194736Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:28.008625547Z","close_reason":"Completed (CLI contract + help text specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23h8","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:33.173157166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-23h8","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:15.117557280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-23h8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Requirements

- Provide a human-friendly help output that reads like a real CLI (`demo_showcase --help`).
- Flags must be reflected in a `Config` struct used by all scenes.

## Suggested flags (baseline)

- `--scene <name>` / `--list-scenes`
- `--quick` / `--speed <f64>`
- `--interactive/--no-interactive`
- `--live/--no-live`
- `--screen/--no-screen`
- `--width <usize>` / `--height <usize>`
- `--force-terminal`
- `--export` / `--export-dir <path>`

## Acceptance criteria

- Unknown flags yield a concise error and `--help` hint.
- `--scene` accepts only known scene names (with a helpful list on error).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rhg","title":"demo_showcase: interactive helpers integration","description":"Use Status/Prompt/Pager to make the demo feel like a real CLI wizard when running in a TTY.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.668932689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917382989Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","interactive"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917352933Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:54.500826925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.684260804Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the showcase feel like a real operator tool, not just output. Use the new interactive helpers *only when safe* (TTY):

- **Prompt**: ask for scenario / speed / whether to export
- **Status**: spinner while “preparing deployment plan” / “running checks”
- **Pager**: show long Markdown (runbook/release notes) without dumping everything into scrollback

## Safety

- If `Console::is_terminal()` is false, Prompt/Pager must not block.
- Provide `--no-interactive` to disable even in a TTY (useful for scripts).

## Acceptance criteria

- Interactive mode improves UX but is never required to run the demo.
- `--no-interactive` yields deterministic non-blocking output.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-3vf2","title":"demo_showcase: advanced content scenes (syntax/markdown/json)","description":"Showcase feature-gated renderables (Syntax/Markdown/Json) with a cohesive narrative (release notes, configs, payloads).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.544872637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:39.937293371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:39.937265379Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:37.537077494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.557411012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Demonstrate the optional, “power-user” renderables in a way that feels like a real CLI tool:

- **Markdown** (`--features markdown` / `full`): show release notes / runbook snippet with headings, lists, code fences.
- **Syntax** (`--features syntax` / `full`): show a config file or code snippet with line numbers and a theme selection.
- **JSON** (`--features json` / `full`): show API request/response payloads with pretty formatting and theming.

## Feature gating behavior

When a feature isn’t enabled, the scene must still run and print a friendly message that includes the exact command to enable it, e.g.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full`

## Acceptance criteria

- With `--features full`, the content scenes look excellent and integrate into the overall narrative (not disconnected examples).
- Without features, output is still polished: a small panel explaining what’s disabled and how to enable it (no `cfg`-related compile failures).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3w3u","title":"demo_showcase: feature-gate messaging","description":"Define exact user-facing messaging for missing features (syntax/markdown/json/tracing) and the commands to enable them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:20.359674542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:54.555986227Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:20.371957033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## User-facing messaging

When a feature is disabled at compile time, the demo should say so clearly and provide an exact command. Example pattern:

> This scene requires the `syntax` feature.
> Run: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full`

Also include `tracing` guidance separately:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing`

## Acceptance criteria

- Messaging is consistent across Markdown/Syntax/JSON/Tracing scenes.
- Output is still “pretty”: use a panel + named styles rather than plain `println!`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4jzg","title":"demo_showcase: Status spinner moments","description":"Use Status spinner for a few short ‘working…’ moments to make the demo feel alive.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:22.516615870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:38.993222298Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","status"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:22.528925448Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Where to use Status

 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-5bjf","title":"demo_showcase: hero + capabilities scene","description":"Implement the hero/capabilities scene: branding, palette preview, environment detection, hyperlinks, emoji.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:06.963744820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:27.536278734Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","hero"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:06.976533058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-5bjf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content requirements

- Big branded title (aligned/centered) + short tagline.
- Capability panel showing:
  - width/height
  - detected color system
  - is_terminal / is_interactive
  - emoji enabled
- Palette preview: small table or columns block showing primary/accent/status colors.
- A hyperlink CTA (docs + repo).

## Acceptance criteria

- This scene alone should convince a user the library is polished.
- Works in non-TTY mode (prints static content; no prompts).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:27Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6ozb","title":"demo_showcase: final polish + QA","description":"Polish pass: visual tweaks, timing, edge cases; ensure all quality gates pass and demo feels ‘product grade’.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.642592479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37.074862135Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","qa"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.660699129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Checklist

- Run full quality gates:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo check --all-targets`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test`
- Visual pass on at least:
  - wide terminal (>= 120 cols)
  - narrow terminal (~70 cols)
  - piped output (`| cat`)
- Ensure the demo ends cleanly: cursor restored, alt-screen disabled, no stray control codes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Demo looks “designed”, not like a pile of examples.
- All scenes have consistent titles, spacing, and style usage.
- Total runtime feels appropriate; `--quick` exists for CI/dev.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-86gp","title":"demo_showcase: capture output for export","description":"Add capture mode to demo runner so scenes can be captured to segments for exporting.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.628166487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:06.942111375Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.642928274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-86gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Approach

- When `--export` or `--export-dir` is set, run the demo with capture enabled:
  - `console.begin_capture()`
  - run selected scenes
  - then call `export_html` / `export_svg`

Be careful: capture + Live/alt-screen don't mix well. For export runs, prefer non-live mode (static output).

## Acceptance criteria

- Export run produces stable artifacts without requiring a TTY.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-abyl","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout fallback (narrow)","description":"Implement a narrow terminal fallback layout (collapse sidebar / stack panels).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.679493487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:25.413885790Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-abyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.704817099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":31,"issue_id":"bd-abyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Narrow-mode behavior

When width is below a threshold (e.g. < 80):
- collapse sidebar beneath main
- reduce table columns / abbreviate labels
- prefer single-column stacking over cramped two-column layout

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics due to zero-width regions.
- Narrow mode still looks intentional and readable.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm
- Unit tests for parsing/validation logic; integration tests for non-interactive fallback
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:10.981531934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:58.940557462Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:58.940491378Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eb17","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:47.247630806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
